item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article could not be sourced and thus is assumed to be non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0). As such, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Somebody help him!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure how I managed to not get this at all the first time around, and it's not really that bad, so I cancelled my downvote. But it's still nothing particularly special, I think, so I won't upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a dumb tearjerker. Pure glurge. It appeals to the basest sentiment. It's pure, unadulterated, sickening pathos with nothing to support it. It's terrible and awful and I'm allergic to schmaltz and that's the only reason I'm tearing up right now. Because of allergies. That +1 is accidental and I will correct it later after I'm done crying for purely involuntarily, medical reasons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I also like this, what if it cycled between all people who had survive to broadcast radiowaves in an apocalypse. Maybe this is just the first dude, maybe there could be more. Not quite as scary but much more mysterious ^^

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a fascinating article. Would love to see more logs to flesh it out but this already deserves a +1 methinks. There are one or two things that need to be cleared up for the sake of other people who want to write similar things. Specifically I'm thinking about where and when this guy is and how the signal gets to us.

Other than that I love it ^^

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This guy seems to be holding on by a thread. His food and ammo are running out and his health is fading (and he's no longer sleeping longer than the duration of a jazz piece). This seems to have been in Foundation hands for a while, so that raised another thought.
What if the anomalous effect tunes in to any of the infinite possible worlds where this guy survived a post-apocalyptic event to broadcast on this frequency? It might even shift between them when he (at least some of him) sleeps. That would allow for his health to have limited effect on the broadcast.
I am not seriously suggesting a link to any GOI, but that would be one hell of a AWCY creation. A radio that endlessly listens to one man on the cusp of dying in a devastated earth, interspersed with solid jazz programming.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is the recording jumping in some parts? I guess that would ruin it a bit and make editing futile. As for the editing, I could do something with audacity, or you do it yourself. Its free and easy. You as voice would work, I guess. It would be the best to ask the author for that part, as to get officially accepted.

If you fix the jumping, please tell me how. I got the same problem.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, I went ahead and did a mock reading- hopefully it's not too bad. If anyone would help me with sound foleying for the effects and proper editing for the video, I'd be happy to do the reading if I'm any good for the part.

http://vocaroo.com/i/s1nvAMRSX1GP

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…So, this somehow isn't an audio log yet?

Upvoted with manly tears.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This DOES feel quite Fallout-esque, but as a standalone post-apocalyptic SCP, it's pretty good. At least dude still has his Jazz. Along with the First Aid and Guns talk, this really evocates an image of a guy that tries to survive, guide and help some of the listening survivors. I like, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sounds like something that could be made into a tale!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You're right- well caught :P The track I was thinking of was Blue in Green, but I seem to have written the album name instead.

EDIT: Fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is so far my favorite SCP. By a significant margin.

Question: 1639-2 refers to a "track" called "Kind of Blue," but there's no such track on the album of that name. Is that supposed to be part of the anomaly — i.e. this is a song that does exist in this alternate universe — or is it indicative of 1639-2's impaired mental function due to hunger/sleep deprivation/sickness/PTSD, or is it a typo? Or am I a jazz nerd who's reading too far into it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wanted to reread this. I discovered it to be at +100. As one of my favorite articles, the fills me with much enjoyment and happiness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is brilliant and made me desperately want to contact the poor guy and let him know someone is listening.

I imagine there's some bleeding-heart doctor in the foundation haunted by this item.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for that, now I'm picturing that and It's even sadder.

Not nice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1639-2's narration occurs at regular intervals regardless of time of day and SCP-1639-2's reported condition.

I missed this before. The fact that the host is implied to go without sleep raises interesting questions about the whole situation…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want to go rescue this guy. Upvoted hard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The piece is very… melancholic. The logs are great, they feel authentic. I like not knowing what happened or what clouders are. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Elegant" is the word that comes to mind. Also, for some reason I imagined this guy in a sort of "last man on Earth" kind of way, as if his radio transmission were Humanity's death rattle. It's hard to pull off this type of subtle premise and not just that, make it good. You totally did it. Kudos! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's the End Of The World As We Know It, things are so desperate that even the supply of baked beans (a resource once thought infinite) is depleted, and the ecosystem is so fragged that not even pigs can find anything to eat…and yet smooth jazz still lingers on.

I can see it now, mutated cockroaches huddled for shelter in a burnt-out VW illuminated only by the flickering light of a cracked radio dial. The giant insect looks at the mother of his brood and shakes his tumor-plagued head. "I can't believe the classic rock station switched to this crap!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This made me sad…up vote for making me feel something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This takes the Jazz Club sketch from the late lamented Fast Show and makes it hauntingly terrifying. Definite upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nope, you're not the only one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would upvote this twice if I could. It's very haunting, the transcripts are very well done. It really gives you this image of a dying man hanging on to sanity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it because it reminds me of that DJ in Downpour that's trapped.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it even without the stinger…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Am I the only one who's pretty meh on this? It just doesn't interest me even slightly. I wouldn't have even gotten it if it weren't for the discussion page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's not a direct Fallout reference, aside from the "post-apocalyptic world, with someone playing older music across the wavelengths for maybe no one to hear" schtick.

That said, I thought it was pretty obvious what was going on…. SOMETHING happened, in another world, maybe another timeline, that has brought about an insane calamity, possibly end-of-the-world but at the very least the breakdown of society in the immediate area. And now this guy's eking out a living, and trying to keep himself from going insane, maybe be one little spark of light in the world… Even as his medical condition/injury (whatever it is) may be slowly killing him… And these clouders (bandit raiders? Aliens? Floating bits of atomic/biological/chemical weapon residue?) are making things even worse…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was confused as to why this had such a high rating until I git the stinger. Nice work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I downvoted before reading the comments, cause I don't get anything from this article except some guy going on about beans and thin pigs. Apparently I'm missing something because the last Fallout I played was 2. I cancelled my vote with the explanation given here, but the logs don't drive the point home nearly as well as they could if you ask me. Anyway, I like the premise, just not the execution.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Poor bastard. :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm guessing that those songs were recorded after the catastrophe in the alternate universe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Most creatures are semi-obviously abnormal. Many (but in no way all or even most ) objects have a historic bit. Still this one is odd. Only one thing, which is exact frequency, cahses anomalies. Granted this in NO way detracts the article to me. Just something I'm bored enough to question.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How'd we find half the stuff on this site?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: random encounter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Loved it.

How did we find this thing though?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
• Firearms

The exact instant this piece pulled a hard left turn and never looked back.

This is just great. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I heard my local Jazz broadcaster in my head.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At least it's not Three-Dog.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually liked this before the log, but the log just made it better. He's trying to survive the apocalypse and all he can do with his spare time is broadcast jazz. My god. Also best when the logs are read in a calm, soothing radio host voice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The majority of the songs heard are pieces known to exist, though a fraction (approximately 4% of studied pieces) have been so far heard exclusively though SCP-1639. These pieces are markedly different, utilising fewer instruments and with lower sound quality.

So is the DJ is some sort of fake alternate universe?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this, absolutely love it. At first I was like "Meh…" but then the logs really brought it out!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How can something be so relaxing and unsettling at the same time? Good shit. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The logs really make this one for me, fantastic work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love it. The pauses in the transcripts are what really do it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh poor guy is trapped in Fallout.
